info.description:
Programmable HTTP reverse proxy. Manage routes, destinations,
listeners, and middlewares via REST API. Changes apply instantly.
info.title: Rutoso API

model.AccessLogConfig.description:
AccessLog configures access logging for this listener.
When nil, no access logs are emitted.

format.description:
Format is the log line format template. Available placeholders depend
on the output mode (see JSON field). When empty, Rutoso uses a
sensible default: "METHOD PATH CLIENT_IP STATUS DURATION_MS BYTES".

json.description: JSON switches the log output to JSON format.

path.description:
Path is the file path where access logs are written.
Use "/dev/stdout" or "stdout" for container-friendly stdout logging.

destinationId.description: DestinationID is the ID of the Destination.

weight.description: Weight controls the proportion of traffic. Must sum to 100 across backends.

model.BalancingOptions.description: Balancing controls the load-balancing algorithm and its parameters.

maglevTableSize.description:
MaglevTableSize sets the Maglev hash table size.
Must be a prime number. Default: 65537. Only used with MAGLEV.

model.CORSConfig.description: CORS holds the CORS filter configuration. Set when Type == "cors".

allowCredentials.description: AllowCredentials indicates whether the request can include user credentials.

allowHeaders.description: AllowHeaders lists the request headers allowed in CORS requests.

allowMethods.description:
AllowMethods lists the HTTP methods allowed in CORS requests.
Example: ["GET", "POST", "OPTIONS"]

allowOrigins.description:
AllowOrigins lists the allowed origin patterns.
Each entry is matched as an exact string or a regex if Regex is true.

exposeHeaders.description: ExposeHeaders lists the response headers the browser is allowed to access.

maxAge.description:
MaxAge sets the preflight cache duration in seconds.
Maps to the Access-Control-Max-Age response header.

regex.description: Regex indicates that Value should be treated as a regular expression.

value.description: Value is the origin string or regex pattern.

model.CircuitBreakerOptions.description: CircuitBreaker limits in-flight traffic to protect the upstream.

maxConnections.description: MaxConnections is the maximum number of concurrent TCP connections.

maxPendingRequests.description: MaxPendingRequests is the maximum number of requests queued.

maxRequests.description: MaxRequests is the maximum number of concurrent requests.

maxRetries.description: MaxRetries is the maximum number of concurrent retries.

host.description:
Host is the upstream FQDN or IP address.
For Kubernetes Services use the full FQDN:
  my-svc.my-namespace.svc.cluster.local

id.description: ID is the unique identifier of this destination.

port.description: Port is the upstream TCP port.

model.DestinationDiscovery.description:
Discovery enables dynamic endpoint resolution.
When nil, Rutoso connects directly to host:port.

model.DestinationOptions.description: Options contains advanced configuration. All fields are optional.

connectTimeout.description:
ConnectTimeout is the timeout for establishing a new TCP connection.
Accepts Go duration strings (e.g. "3s", "500ms"). Default: 5s.

http2.description: HTTP2 enables HTTP/2 to the upstream. Required for gRPC backends.

maxRequestsPerConnection.description:
MaxRequestsPerConnection drains a connection after this many requests.
0 means unlimited.

model.DiscoveryType.description: Type selects the discovery mechanism. Currently only "kubernetes".

model.ExtAuthzConfig.description:
ExtAuthz holds the external authorisation filter configuration.
Set when Type == "extAuthz".

allowedClientHeaders.description:
AllowedClientHeaders lists header patterns from the authz response
that are returned to the client when the authz service denies.

allowedHeaders.description:
AllowedHeaders lists header patterns from the client request that are
forwarded to the authz service. Host, Method, Path, Content-Length,
and Authorization are always included automatically.

allowedUpstreamHeaders.description:
AllowedUpstreamHeaders lists header patterns from the authz response
that are added to the original client request (forwarded to upstream)
when the authz service returns 200 OK.

destinationId.description:
DestinationID references the Destination entity that hosts the
authorisation service. Rutoso builds the full server_uri from the
Destination's host, port, and TLS config automatically.

failureModeAllow.description:
FailureModeAllow controls what happens when the authz service is
unreachable. If true, requests are allowed through (fail-open).
Default is false (fail-closed).

headersToAdd.description:
HeadersToAdd are extra headers injected into the check request sent
to the authz service.

includeRequestBodyInCheck.description: IncludeRequestBodyInCheck forwards the request body to the authz service.

mode.description:
Mode selects the transport protocol to the authz service.
"grpc" or "http". Default: "http".

path.description:
Path is the path of the authorization endpoint on the service.
Example: "/oauth2/auth". Rutoso appends it to the Destination's
host:port to build the full server_uri automatically.
Ignored in gRPC mode.

timeout.description: Timeout is the authorisation request deadline (e.g. "5s", "500ms").

model.ExtProcConfig.description:
ExtProc holds the external processing filter configuration.
Set when Type == "extProc".

destinationId.description:
DestinationID references the Destination entity that hosts the
external processing service. Must be a gRPC service.

timeout.description: Timeout is the processing request deadline (e.g. "2s").

model.ExtProcMode.description:
ProcessingMode controls which parts of the HTTP transaction are sent to
the external processor.

model.ExtProcPhase.description: ResponseBodyMode controls processing of the response body.

model.ForwardAction.description:
Forward instructs Envoy to proxy the request to one or more upstream
Destinations. Contains all forwarding behaviour: backends, timeouts,
retries, URL rewriting, and traffic mirroring.
Mutually exclusive with Redirect and DirectResponse.

backends.description:
Backends lists the upstream Destinations for this route.
Each entry references a Destination by ID and carries a traffic weight.
Weights across all backends must sum to 100 when more than one backend
is defined. If only one backend is provided its weight is ignored.

hashPolicy.description:
HashPolicy controls how Envoy computes the consistent-hash key for
sticky sessions. Only relevant when the Destination uses RING_HASH or
MAGLEV balancing (configured in Destination.Options.Balancing).

This field lives on the route — not on the Destination — because Envoy
evaluates hash_policy at routing time using request attributes (headers,
cookies, client IP) that are only available in the RouteAction context.
The Destination defines *which algorithm* and ring parameters to use;
the route defines *what request data* feeds the hash function.
Both sides must be configured for sticky sessions to work.

Entries are evaluated in order; the first one that produces a value wins.
Maps to RouteAction.hash_policy in Envoy.

maxGrpcTimeout.description:
MaxGRPCTimeout caps the timeout that a gRPC client can request via
the grpc-timeout header. If the client asks for more, Envoy clamps
it to this value. Accepts Go duration strings (e.g. "30s").
Maps to RouteAction.max_grpc_timeout.

websocket.description:
Websocket enables HTTP/1.1 WebSocket upgrade for this route.
When true, Envoy allows clients to upgrade the connection to a
WebSocket and proxies frames bidirectionally to the upstream.
Maps to RouteAction.upgrade_configs with type "websocket".

cookie.description: Cookie uses the named cookie as the hash key.

cookieTtl.description: CookieTTL is the TTL for auto-generated sticky cookies. Accepts Go durations.

header.description: Header uses the named request header as the hash key.

sourceIP.description: SourceIP uses the client IP as the hash key.

regex.description: Regex indicates that Value should be treated as a regular expression.

value.description:
Value is the exact value the header must have.
If empty, only the presence of the header is checked.

append.description:
Append controls whether the header is appended (true) or replaced
(false) if it already exists. Default: true.

model.HeadersConfig.description:
Headers holds the header mutation filter configuration.
Set when Type == "headers".

requestHeadersToAdd.description:
RequestHeadersToAdd are headers added to the request before forwarding
to the upstream.

requestHeadersToRemove.description:
RequestHeadersToRemove are header names removed from the request
before forwarding.

responseHeadersToAdd.description:
ResponseHeadersToAdd are headers added to the response before
returning to the client.

responseHeadersToRemove.description:
ResponseHeadersToRemove are header names removed from the response
before returning to the client.

model.HealthCheckOptions.description: HealthCheck configures active HTTP health checking.

healthyThreshold.description: HealthyThreshold is consecutive successes before marking healthy. Default: 2.

interval.description: Interval is how often health checks run. Default: "10s".

path.description: Path is the HTTP path for health-check requests. Required.

timeout.description: Timeout is how long to wait for a response. Default: "5s".

unhealthyThreshold.description: UnhealthyThreshold is consecutive failures before marking unhealthy. Default: 3.

claim.description: Claim is the JWT claim name (e.g. "sub", "email").

header.description: Header is the request header name that receives the claim value.

model.JWTConfig.description: JWT holds the JWT authn filter configuration. Set when Type == "jwt".

providers.description:
Providers is a map of provider name to JWTProvider configuration.
The map key is referenced from per-route overrides to select a specific
provider (or disable authentication for that route).

rules.description:
Rules defines which request paths require JWT validation and which
provider to apply. If empty, all paths are validated by all providers.

audiences.description:
Audiences lists the expected "aud" claim values. If empty, audience
validation is skipped.

claimToHeaders.description:
ClaimToHeaders maps JWT claim names to upstream request header names.
The claim value is set as the header value before forwarding.

forwardJwt.description:
ForwardJWT indicates whether the original Authorization header should be
forwarded to the upstream after successful validation.

issuer.description: Issuer is the expected value of the "iss" claim.

jwksDestinationId.description:
JWKsDestinationID references the Destination entity that hosts the
JWKS endpoint. Required when JWKsURI is set. The Destination's cluster
(with its TLS config) is used as the upstream for fetching keys.

jwksInline.description:
JWKsInline is a literal JSON Web Key Set document.
Mutually exclusive with JWKsURI.

jwksUri.description:
JWKsURI is the URL path from which the JSON Web Key Set is fetched.
When set, JWKsDestinationID must also be set to identify the upstream
that serves the JWKS endpoint.
Mutually exclusive with JWKsInline.

allowMissing.description:
AllowMissing allows requests without a JWT token to pass through.
Useful for public endpoints that coexist in the same listener.

match.description: Match is the path prefix this rule applies to.

requires.description:
Requires lists the provider names that must validate the request.
All listed providers must succeed (AND semantics).

model.LBPolicy.description: Algorithm selects the load-balancing policy. Default: ROUND_ROBIN.

address.description:
Address is the IP address the listener binds to.
Defaults to "0.0.0.0" if empty.

http2.description:
HTTP2 enables HTTP/2 on this listener. Required for gRPC clients.
With TLS, Go enables HTTP/2 automatically. Without TLS (h2c), Rutoso
enables h2c upgrade support.

id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the listener.

maxRequestHeadersKB.description:
MaxRequestHeadersKB limits the total size of request headers in
kilobytes. Requests exceeding this limit receive a 431 response.
Default: 0 (no limit).

name.description: Name is a human-readable label for the listener.

port.description: Port is the TCP port the listener binds to.

proxyProtocol.description:
ProxyProtocol enables PROXY protocol v1/v2 parsing. Required when
the listener is behind a load balancer that sends PROXY protocol
to communicate the real client IP.

serverName.description:
ServerName sets the "Server" response header.
When empty, no Server header is added.

model.ListenerTLS.description:
TLS holds optional TLS termination configuration.
When nil, the listener operates in plaintext mode.

certPath.description: CertPath is the path to the PEM-encoded TLS certificate file.

keyPath.description: KeyPath is the path to the PEM-encoded private key file.

maxVersion.description:
MaxVersion is the maximum TLS protocol version to accept.
Accepted values: same as MinVersion. If empty, no upper bound is set.

minVersion.description:
MinVersion is the minimum TLS protocol version to accept.
Accepted values: "TLSv1_0", "TLSv1_1", "TLSv1_2", "TLSv1_3".
Defaults to "TLSv1_2" if empty.

model.MatchRule.description: Match defines the conditions that a request must satisfy.

grpc.description:
GRPC restricts this match to gRPC requests only (content-type
application/grpc). Maps to RouteMatch.grpc.

headers.description: Headers are request header matchers that must all match.

hostnames.description:
Hostnames restricts the match to specific virtual host names.
An empty slice matches all virtual hosts.

methods.description:
Methods lists the HTTP methods this rule applies to.
An empty slice matches all methods.

path.description: Path is the exact path that must match.

pathPrefix.description:
PathPrefix is a prefix that the request path must start with.
Mutually exclusive with Path and PathRegex.

pathRegex.description:
PathRegex is a regular expression the request path must match.
Mutually exclusive with Path and PathPrefix.

queryParams.description: QueryParams are query parameter matchers that must all match.

id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the filter.

name.description: Name is a human-readable label for the filter.

disabled.description:
Disabled completely disables the filter for this route/group.
When true, no other field is evaluated.

extAuthzContextExtensions.description:
ExtAuthzContextExtensions adds key/value pairs to the ext_authz check request.
Only meaningful when the referenced filter is of type "extAuthz".

jwtProvider.description:
JWTProvider selects a specific JWT provider by name (instead of requiring all).
Only meaningful when the referenced filter is of type "jwt".

model.MiddlewareType.description:
Type identifies which Envoy HTTP filter this entity configures.
Exactly one of the Config* fields below must match this type.

model.OutlierDetectionOptions.description:
OutlierDetection automatically ejects endpoints that return
consecutive errors, without requiring active health checks.

baseEjectionTime.description: BaseEjectionTime is how long an endpoint stays ejected. Default: "30s".

consecutive5xx.description: Consecutive5xx is consecutive 5xx responses that trigger ejection. Default: 5.

consecutiveGatewayErrors.description: ConsecutiveGatewayErrors is consecutive 502/503/504 that trigger ejection.

interval.description: Interval is how often ejection conditions are evaluated. Default: "10s".

maxEjectionPercent.description: MaxEjectionPercent is the maximum percentage of endpoints ejected. Default: 10.

name.description: Name is the query parameter name.

regex.description: Regex indicates that Value should be treated as a regular expression.

value.description: Value is the exact value the parameter must have.

model.RateLimitConfig.description:
RateLimit holds the rate limit filter configuration.
Set when Type == "rateLimit".

burst.description:
Burst is the maximum number of requests allowed in a burst above the
sustained rate. Default: same as RequestsPerSecond.

requestsPerSecond.description:
RequestsPerSecond is the sustained rate of requests allowed per client IP.
Default: 10.

model.RetryBackoff.description: Backoff controls the delay between retry attempts.

base.description:
Base is the initial delay before the first retry.
Accepts Go duration strings (e.g. "100ms").

max.description:
Max is the upper bound on the backoff delay.
Accepts Go duration strings (e.g. "1s").

model.RewriteRegex.description: PathRegex rewrites the path using a regular expression substitution.

pattern.description: Pattern is the RE2 regular expression matched against the request path.

substitution.description:
Substitution is the replacement string. Capture groups from Pattern
can be referenced as \1, \2, etc.

model.RingSizeOptions.description: RingSize tunes the consistent hash ring. Only used with RING_HASH.

max.description: Max is the maximum number of virtual nodes. Default: 8388608.

min.description: Min is the minimum number of virtual nodes. Default: 1024.

id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the route.

middlewareIds.description:
MiddlewareIDs lists the IDs of Middleware entities active on this route.
The builder registers these in the Envoy HCM pipeline and enables them
only for this route (other routes where the middleware is not listed
get it disabled via per_filter_config).

middlewareOverrides.description:
MiddlewareOverrides carries per-route overrides for active middlewares.
The map key is the Middleware ID. When both the route's group and the
route itself carry an override for the same middleware, the route
override wins entirely (more specific takes precedence).

name.description: Name is a human-readable label for the route.

model.RouteDirectResponse.description:
DirectResponse instructs Envoy to return a fixed HTTP response without
contacting any upstream. Useful for health-check endpoints, maintenance
pages, or returning a static 404.
Mutually exclusive with Forward and Redirect.

body.description: Body is the response body returned to the client. Optional.

status.description: Status is the HTTP status code to return. Required.

headers.description: Headers are appended to each referenced route's own header matchers.

hostnames.description:
Hostnames are merged (union) with each referenced route's own hostnames.
An empty slice means only the route's own hostnames apply.

id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the group.

includeAttemptCount.description:
IncludeAttemptCount makes Envoy add the x-envoy-attempt-count header
to upstream requests, indicating how many times the request has been
attempted (including the original). Maps to
VirtualHost.include_request_attempt_count.

middlewareIds.description:
MiddlewareIDs lists the IDs of Middleware entities active on all routes
in this group. The builder registers these in the Envoy HCM pipeline
and enables them for every route in the group.

middlewareOverrides.description:
MiddlewareOverrides carries per-group overrides for active middlewares.
The map key is the Middleware ID. These overrides apply to all routes
in the group. If a route also carries an override for the same
middleware, the route override wins entirely.

name.description: Name is a human-readable label for the group.

pathPrefix.description:
PathPrefix is prepended literally to the path/pathPrefix/pathRegex of
every referenced route. Mutually exclusive with PathRegex.

pathRegex.description:
PathRegex is a RE2 regular expression that defines the group's path
namespace. It is composed with the route's own path specifier according
to the rules described above. Mutually exclusive with PathPrefix.

routeIds.description:
RouteIDs lists the IDs of the routes that belong to this group.
Routes are independent entities and may be referenced by multiple groups.

destinationId.description:
DestinationID is the ID of the Destination that receives the
mirrored traffic.

percentage.description:
Percentage is the fraction of requests to mirror, from 0 to 100.
Default: 100 (mirror all matched traffic).

model.RouteRedirect.description:
Redirect instructs Envoy to return an HTTP redirect to the client
instead of forwarding to an upstream.
Mutually exclusive with Forward and DirectResponse.

code.description:
Code is the HTTP status code returned to the client.
Accepted values: 301, 302, 303, 307, 308. Default: 301.

host.description: Host overrides only the hostname in the redirect target.

path.description: Path replaces the path component of the redirect target.

stripQuery.description: StripQuery removes the query string from the redirect target.

url.description:
URL is the complete target URL. When set, Scheme, Host, Path, and
StripQuery are ignored — the client is sent directly to this URL.

model.RouteRetry.description: Retry controls automatic retry behaviour when the upstream fails.

attempts.description:
Attempts is the maximum number of times the request is retried.
The original request does not count — setting 3 means up to 3 retries
after the first failure (4 total attempts).

on.description:
On lists the conditions that trigger a retry. When empty, defaults
to ["server-error", "connection-failure"].

perAttemptTimeout.description:
PerAttemptTimeout is the deadline for each individual attempt.
Accepts Go duration strings (e.g. "5s").

retriableCodes.description:
RetriableCodes is the explicit list of HTTP status codes that trigger
a retry. Only evaluated when On contains "retriable-codes".

model.RouteRewrite.description: Rewrite transforms the URL before sending the request upstream.

autoHost.description:
AutoHost sets the Host header to the hostname of the upstream
Destination automatically. Useful when the backend requires its own
hostname (e.g. an external SaaS API).

host.description: Host overrides the Host header sent to the upstream with a fixed value.

hostFromHeader.description:
HostFromHeader takes the value of the named request header and uses it
as the Host header sent to the upstream.

path.description:
Path replaces the matched path prefix with the given value.
For example, if the route matches "/api/v1" and Path is "/internal",
a request to "/api/v1/users" arrives at the backend as "/internal/users".

model.RouteTimeouts.description: Timeouts controls how long the request is allowed to take.

idle.description:
Idle is the maximum time a connection may remain open with no data
flowing. Accepts Go duration strings. Useful for long-lived streaming
connections that may stall.

request.description:
Request is the total time the entire request may take from the moment
Envoy receives the first byte from the client until the response is
fully sent. Accepts Go duration strings (e.g. "30s", "1m").

model.TLSMode.description: Mode selects the connection security model. Default: none (plaintext).

model.TLSOptions.description: TLS controls upstream TLS / mTLS configuration.

caFile.description:
CAFile is the path to the CA certificate PEM file. When empty,
the system CA bundle (/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt) is used.

certFile.description:
CertFile is the path to the client certificate PEM file.
Required when Mode is mtls.

keyFile.description:
KeyFile is the path to the client private key PEM file.
Required when Mode is mtls.

maxVersion.description: MaxVersion is the maximum TLS protocol version.

minVersion.description:
MinVersion is the minimum TLS protocol version.
Accepted values: "TLSv1_0", "TLSv1_1", "TLSv1_2", "TLSv1_3".

sni.description:
SNI overrides the Server Name Indication sent during TLS handshake.
When empty, the destination host is used.

error.description: Error contains a human-readable description of what went wrong.

get.description: Returns the full list of destinations.

post.description: Creates a new upstream destination entity.

delete.description: Deletes the destination with the given ID.

get.description: Returns the destination with the given ID.

put.description: Replaces the destination with the given ID.

get.description: Returns the full list of route groups.

post.description: Creates a new group referencing existing routes by ID.

delete.description: Deletes the group with the given ID.

get.description: Returns the group with the given ID.

put.description: Replaces the group with the given ID.

get.description: Returns the full list of listeners.

post.description: Creates a new Envoy listener entity.

delete.description: Deletes the listener with the given ID.

get.description: Returns the listener with the given ID.

put.description: Replaces the listener with the given ID.

get.description: Returns the full list of middlewares.

post.description: Creates a new HTTP mw entity.

delete.description: Deletes the mw with the given ID.

get.description: Returns the mw with the given ID.

put.description: Replaces the mw with the given ID.

get.description: Returns the full list of routes.

delete.description: Deletes the route with the given ID.

get.description: Returns the route with the given ID.

put.description: Replaces the route with the given ID.
